description: Add a participant-facing panel at /user/ showing real-time device metrics and a CSV/JSON data export. Role is read from Clerk publicMetadata.role — admins go to /panel/, everyone else goes to /user/. Frontend-only with mock data.

userStories.title: Expose landing color tokens as Tailwind utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to use bg-foam, text-lagoon, text-sea-ink etc. as Tailwind classes so the user panel shares the landing page's visual language without arbitrary value syntax.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/styles.css, add the following entries inside the existing @theme inline block, after the --color-sidebar-ring line: --color-foam: var(--foam); --color-sand: var(--sand); --color-lagoon: var(--lagoon); --color-lagoon-deep: var(--lagoon-deep); --color-sea-ink: var(--sea-ink); --color-sea-ink-soft: var(--sea-ink-soft); --color-palm: var(--palm);
Existing --foam, --sand, --lagoon etc. CSS variable definitions in :root and .dark blocks are left untouched
After the change, Tailwind classes bg-foam, text-lagoon, text-sea-ink, bg-sand, border-sea-ink are usable in TSX files
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Role-based routing guard in _authenticated layout
userStories.description: As the system, I want authenticated users redirected to the correct panel based on their Clerk role so admins never land on the user panel and participants never land on the admin panel.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/routes/_authenticated.tsx, import useUser from @clerk/react and useNavigate, useRouterState from @tanstack/react-router
Read role via user?.publicMetadata?.role (cast as string | undefined)

Inside a useEffect with [isLoaded, user, navigate] dependencies: if isLoaded and path starts with /panel and role is not 'admin', call navigate({ to: '/user/' }); if isLoaded and path starts with /user and role === 'admin', call navigate({ to: '/panel/' })
Use useRouterState to get the current pathname for the path checks
While !isLoaded, render a full-screen centered div with a single div using classes animate-spin rounded-full h-8 w-8 border-2 border-primary border-t-transparent
Paths other than /panel and /user (e.g. landing, sign-in) are not redirected
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: User panel route and layout shell
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the /user/ route to exist with a full page layout shell so the device and export sections can be dropped in.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/routes/_authenticated/user/index.tsx with createFileRoute('/_authenticated/user/')
Page root is a div with classes min-h-screen bg-foam

Header bar contains: left side — span with classes text-lg font-bold text-lagoon reading 'PulseLab'; center — greeting 'Olá, [firstName]' using useUser() from @clerk/react with classes text-sm text-sea-ink; right side — UserButtonData component imported from @/components/user/UserButtonData

Below header, a main section with classes p-6 grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-6 containing two shadcn Card components: left card with className col-span-3 and CardTitle 'Meus Dispositivos'; right card with className col-span-2 and CardTitle 'Exportar Dados'
CardTitle elements use className text-sea-ink
Card bodies show placeholder text 'Em breve...' in text-muted-foreground
Zero inline styles
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Device status and real-time metrics display
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my device's current status and live metrics so I know my wearable is working and my data is being collected.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/user-panel/DeviceCard.tsx with props interface { device: Device } imported from @types

Top section shows: device model in font-semibold text-sea-ink, device ID in text-xs text-muted-foreground, StatusPill imported from @/components/panel/StatusPill, battery percentage with Lucide Battery icon, last sync time with Lucide RefreshCw icon — all Tailwind classes, zero inline styles
Below device info, a Separator from shadcn ui, then a grid grid-cols-2 gap-3 of four metric tiles
Each metric tile is a div with classes rounded-lg border border-border bg-background p-3 flex flex-col gap-1 border-l-2 border-l-lagoon

Each tile shows: a div with classes flex items-center gap-1 mb-1 containing a span with classes inline-block h-2 w-2 rounded-full bg-lagoon animate-pulse and the metric label in text-xs text-muted-foreground; the value in text-2xl font-bold text-sea-ink; the unit in text-xs text-muted-foreground
Four tiles in order: HR (device.hr, unit 'bpm'), HRV (device.hrv, unit 'ms'), SpO2 (device.spo2, unit '%'), Stress (device.stress, unit '/100'); null values render as '--'
In src/routes/_authenticated/user/index.tsx: import DEVICES from @api/data/mockData and DeviceCard; replace 'Em breve...' in the left card with <DeviceCard device={DEVICES[0]} />
Zero inline styles
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Export panel with mock CSV and JSON download
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select a date range, granularity, and format to download my collected data so I can analyze it externally.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/user-panel/ExportPanel.tsx with no props
Local state: startDate and endDate as strings defaulting to today in YYYY-MM-DD format, granularity as 'hour' | 'day' defaulting to 'hour', format as 'csv' | 'json' defaulting to 'csv'
Two shadcn Input type='date' fields labeled 'De' and 'Até' wired to startDate and endDate state
A shadcn Select for granularity with two options: value='hour' label='Por hora', value='day' label='Por dia'
Two shadcn Button components side by side for format: 'CSV' and 'JSON'; active one uses variant='default', inactive uses variant='outline'
A full-width shadcn Button variant='default' with Lucide Download icon and text 'Exportar' that calls handleExport on click

handleExport: iterates from new Date(startDate) to new Date(endDate) by 3600000ms (hour) or 86400000ms (day); each row is { timestamp: isoString, deviceId: 'GW-0041', hr: randomInt(55,100), hrv: randomInt(20,60), spo2: randomInt(95,100), stress: randomInt(10,80) }; capped at 500 rows
CSV export: header row 'timestamp,deviceId,hr,hrv,spo2,stress' + data rows joined by newlines; Blob type 'text/csv'; filename 'pulselab-export.csv'
JSON export: JSON.stringify(rows, null, 2); Blob type 'application/json'; filename 'pulselab-export.json'
Download via: const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob); const a = document.createElement('a'); a.href = url; a.download = filename; a.click(); URL.revokeObjectURL(url)
In src/routes/_authenticated/user/index.tsx: import ExportPanel and replace 'Em breve...' in the right card with <ExportPanel />
Zero inline styles
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
